subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Autoimmune Diseases
description: A quick, practical guide to Autoimmune Diseases.

hook.text: Your immune system is designed to attack invaders. In autoimmune disease, it attacks you instead.

buildup.text: Autoimmune diseases occur when the immune system mistakes the body's own cells as foreign threats.

discovery.text: Type 1 diabetes destroys insulin-producing cells. Rheumatoid arthritis attacks joint tissue.

twist.text: Women are 3 times more likely than men to develop autoimmune diseases—the reason is still unclear.

climax.text: Over 80 autoimmune diseases exist, affecting 24 million Americans combined.

punchline.text: The immune system's greatest failure: too good at its job, targeting itself.

quiz.question: What happens in an autoimmune disease?
options: The immune system attacks the body's own healthy cells
The immune system becomes too weak to fight infection
A virus permanently damages immune cells
